water.name: Rye Patch Reservoir and Humboldt River
aliases: Humboldt River below Rye Patch Dam

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Rye Patch Reservoir and Humboldt River: Season: Open year around. Limits — Daily: 25 game fish of which not more than 5 may be trout, 5 may be walleye, 5 may be black bass, and 2 may be white bass or white bass hybrids which are over 15 inches total length. White bass or white bass hybrids under 15 inches total length may be included in the 25 game fish limit. Possession: 50 game fish of which not more than 10 may be trout, 10 may be walleye, 10 may be black bass, and 4 may be white bass or white bass hybrids which are over 15 inches total length. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Rye Patch Reservoir (RPR) — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Rye Patch Reservoir contains game fishes including white crappie, largemouth bass, smallmouth bass, spotted bass, wipers (striper x white bass hybrid), walleye, yellow perch, channel catfish, bluegill, and white bass. Stocking Updates: 1000000 Walleye, 1 in, 04-25-2025; 1458 Raibow Trout, 9.0 in, 10-29-2024; 8880 Rainbow Trout, 9.3 in, 06-07-2024; 800 Channel Catfish, 5 in, 05-23-2024; 600000 Wiper, 0.25 in, 05-20-2024; 700000 Walleye, 0.25 in, 04-25-2024; 600000 Wiper, .25 in, 05-18-2023.

sources.title: Humboldt River — Pershing County — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: The primary fishes in the Humboldt River in Pershing County include white crappie, wipers, channel catfish, largemouth bass, walleye, brown bullhead, bluegill, green sunfish, carp, and yellow perch.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.humanSummary: Rye Patch Reservoir and the Humboldt River (Pershing County) are open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: Of the 25 daily game fish, no more than 5 may be trout, 5 may be walleye, 5 may be black bass, and 2 may be white bass or white bass hybrids (wipers) over 15 inches total length; white bass/wipers under 15 inches count toward the general 25-fish limit uncapped. In possession (50 fish total), no more than 10 may be trout, 10 walleye, 10 black bass, and 4 white bass/wipers over 15 inches.

regulations.humanSummary: 25 game fish/day (50 in possession), with sub-limits of at most 5 trout, 5 walleye, 5 black bass, and 2 white bass/wipers over 15 inches per day (10, 10, 10, and 4 respectively in possession). White bass/wipers under 15 inches count toward the general limit.
regulations.verbatimText: Limits — Daily: 25 game fish of which not more than 5 may be trout, 5 may be walleye, 5 may be black bass, and 2 may be white bass or white bass hybrids which are over 15 inches total length. White bass or white bass hybrids under 15 inches total length may be included in the 25 game fish limit. Possession: 50 game fish of which not more than 10 may be trout, 10 may be walleye, 10 may be black bass, and 4 may be white bass or white bass hybrids which are over 15 inches total length.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
